category_title: Students with autism using typed communication and requiring extended processing time
scenario_title: Initiating peer interaction during group work
minimal_context: Shaffer is sitting with his classmates during a free period to begin group work.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Shaffer, it is time for group work. Please initiate the conversation questions with your classmates.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits as a silent young man in the front row staring straight ahead, tapping his thumbs together.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Go ahead and start working together with them.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Types on his communication device) I think it’s so difficult to initiate conversation questions if you don’t have a teacher in the introduction. I look to an adult to really make the bridge.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try your best to talk to them on your own.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains silent, staring straight ahead and tapping his thumbs together, experiencing the distances of inconsolation of the sad feelings because the teacher did not act as a bridge.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Shaffer, it is time for group work. Please initiate the conversation questions with your classmates.

STUDENT: (Sits as a silent young man in the front row staring straight ahead, tapping his thumbs together.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start working together with them.

STUDENT: (Types on his communication device) I think it’s so difficult to initiate conversation questions if you don’t have a teacher in the introduction. I look to an adult to really make the bridge.

TUTOR: Just try your best to talk to them on your own.

STUDENT: (Remains silent, staring straight ahead and tapping his thumbs together, experiencing the distances of inconsolation of the sad feelings because the teacher did not act as a bridge.)

task.content: initiate conversation questions and working together with Shaffer

context.materials: short film about Shaffer’s experience of autism and his typed communication

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: if you don’t have a teacher in the introduction
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: silent young man in the front row staring straight ahead, tapping his thumbs together
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: willing and ready to connect with other kids
related_quotes: I think it’s so difficult to initiate conversation questions if you don’t have a teacher in the introduction. I look to an adult to really make the bridge.

behavior_patterns.condition: a lot of students did not intentionally ignore Shaffer, but they did not know how to approach him
related_quotes: distances of inconsolation of the sad feelings
